timeline.label: Q1 2026: revenue $663M (+69% YoY), DAUq 126.8M (+17%), ARPU $5.23 (+44%) — monetization acceleration
FY2025 10-K: first full profitable year — net income $529.7M, FCF $684.2M, revenue $2.20B
Expanded data licensing partnerships for AI training (other revenue stream)
FY2024 10-K filed: net loss $(484.3)M; transition year post-IPO
CEO Steven Huffman SC 13D/A amendment — post-IPO beneficial ownership update
CEO Steven Huffman SC 13D at IPO — insider alignment signal
IPO pricing and NYSE listing — Class A shares begin trading
Ad platform rebuild and automation — foundation for ARPU expansion post-2023
WallStreetBets / meme-stock visibility spike — DAU elevated then normalized
Steven Huffman return as CEO (2015 onward) — product-led culture persists
Redesign and mobile-first push — modern app foundation

industry.structure: Digital advertising and social platforms are oligopolistic at scale (Meta, Google, TikTok), but topic-based communities occupy a niche with differentiated intent data. Barriers are moderate: network effects within subreddits, but multi-homing is easy.
industry.share_shift: Reddit is gaining monetization yield (ARPU +44% Q1 2026) faster than pure user share versus mega-cap peers; search and AI licensing are incremental share grabs in attention and data markets.
industry.trend: AI training data licensing, retail-investor community engagement, and automated ad buying are reshaping revenue mix; moderation and bot detection remain operational priorities.
